codeReview.summary: The worker discovered that repository-level memory scoping was already implemented in the codebase. The implementation includes: (1) repository field in MemoryEntry struct, (2) repository field required in MemoryQuery struct, (3) all SQL queries filter by repository with WHERE repository = ?, and (4) methods like get_by_type, get_by_label, get_by_provenance all filter by repository. The worker created 9 comprehensive integration tests in crates/swell-memory/tests/repo_scoping.rs covering all VAL-MEM-007 pass conditions. All tests pass and clippy is clean.

issues.description: Validation contract specifies running 'cargo test -p swell-memory -- memory_scope' but the worker created tests under 'repo_scoping' test binary. The test file name is consistent with the feature name 'mem-repo-scoping' and tests verify the correct behavior, so this is a naming discrepancy rather than a functional issue.

summary: PASS - Repository-level memory scoping feature verified. The existing implementation correctly tags all memory entries with repository context and filters all memory queries by scope. Created 9 comprehensive integration tests covering: store/get with repo context, search with repo scope, cross-repo isolation (3 repos), get_by_type, get_by_label, skills/patterns isolation, text search boundaries, and repo scope with/without query text. All tests pass (9 integration + 425 unit), clippy clean.
